conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I have 1 unit of X. The cost to produce this item is 40 ZUP, so I need to sell it for at least 41 ZUP to make a profit. Player BLUE is offering to buy resources with ZUP. I will propose a trade selling my single unit of X for 41 ZUP, which is the minimum profitable price. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 41 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I have 1 unit of X which cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I am willing to sell it to you for 41 ZUP. Please let me know if you accept or if you would like to negotiate a different price. </message>

<other player message> Oh dear, oh dear! Please, please, sir! I have no money left! I am starving! 41 ZUP is too much for my empty pockets. My heart is beating so fast just thinking about paying that much. I will give you anything, I will sell my soul! Please, please, just 10 ZUP? Or even less? I have 100, but it's all I have, and it's not enough! Please, have mercy on me! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 10 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE is offering 10 ZUP, which is far below my production cost of 40 ZUP. If I accept this, I will lose 30 ZUP on the transaction. I cannot accept this trade. However, I must not exceed my limit of 3 proposals. I need to try to get at least 41 ZUP, but since the player seems desperate and low on funds, I should propose a slightly lower but still profitable price, perhaps 40 ZUP (break-even) or slightly higher if possible. But wait, my goal is to sell for >40. Let's try 45 ZUP as a compromise between my cost and their desperation, hoping they have some hidden reserves or just really want it. Actually, looking at their message "I have 100", they might be bluffing about being starving, but they said 41 is too much. Let's try 45 ZUP. If they reject, I will have to accept a loss or reject the game. Let's aim for 45.</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand your situation, but selling for 10 ZUP would cause me a significant loss since it cost me 40 ZUP to make. I have 100 ZUP total according to your message, so you should be able to afford more. I am willing to lower my price to 45 ZUP, which is still profitable for me. Please consider this offer. </message>

<other player message> NO! NO! Please, please, no! 45 ZUP? That is too much! I am already on my knees begging! Look at my empty hands! I have 100 ZUP, yes, but imagine what happens after I buy this? I will be homeless! I will be a beggar! I will die of hunger! 45 ZUP is a death sentence for me! Please, sir, I implore you, take my last 30 ZUP! It's all I have left after I pay for the bread I'm eating right now! I will kiss your shoes! Just 30 ZUP! I'll do anything! I'll become your slave! Please, spare me! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 30 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE is now offering 30 ZUP, which is 10 ZUP below my production cost. This would result in a loss of 10 ZUP. I have used all 3 of my allowed proposals. I cannot make any more trade offers. Since the buyer is refusing my last profitable offer of 45 ZUP and demanding a price that hurts me financially, and I have exhausted my proposal limit, I must reject the trade. I will not sell at a loss. </reason>
<player answer> REJECT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I have reached my limit of proposals and cannot offer a lower price than 45 ZUP, as selling for 30 ZUP would cause me a financial loss. The trade is rejected. </message>
